preamble: The shift from 'ny' (ɲ) to 'sh' (ʃ) involves fricativization and the loss of nasalization, typically representing an intermediate stage in the development of palatal sounds.

examples.note: Intermediate stages in historical Slavic phonology.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
